title: H-A2 stricter original-entry robustness and prioritization preregistration
purpose: Pre-register a stricter local no-paid review of the H-A2.32 timestamp-clean original-entry signal before any E2, exact replay, paid data, IBKR request, LLM call, paper trading, or operational claim.
source_decision_summary: H-A2.32 produced E1 diagnostic evidence: the 09:35-only rule is timestamp-clean, excludes the 15-minute conflict component, keeps threshold 0.001 locked, and retains 14 OOS trade days. The result remains under-sampled and underpowered, so the next safe step is robustness/prioritization review or independent validation-data planning.
research_question: Is the H-A2.32 original-entry signal robust enough to prioritize independent validation planning, or is it too sample-fragile, regime-concentrated, or skip-cost-dependent to pursue further before new data?

allowed_inputs.use: source retained/skipped train and OOS groups from the pre-registered 09:35-only rule
verify no new threshold or OOS-selected filter was introduced
classify existing days into already-available deterministic regime buckets only
compare original-entry retained/skipped groups to existing implementable trade outcomes and proxy context

forbidden_inputs.reason: This review decides whether validation data is worth planning; it does not buy data.
Exact 2022 replay remains a separate blocked branch.
H-L1 remains blocked until real timestamp-clean news cases exist.
News-Unblock remains under cooldown and is unrelated to this local H-A2 review.
Would convert robustness review into leakage-prone OOS tuning.

planned_future_tests.question: Does the review preserve the exact H-A2.32 rule without threshold changes, OOS tuning, delayed-entry logic, or the 15-minute conflict component?
Does retained OOS performance survive removing the most influential retained day and the most extreme 5% of retained/skipped days where sample size allows?
Is the retained signal concentrated in one calendar month, weekday, volatility bucket, or macro/VIX regime?
Does the retained group improvement justify the number and PnL of skipped trades without hiding losses through sample collapse?
Should H-A2 prioritize independent validation-data planning, run another local diagnostic, return to delayed-entry quote acquisition, or park the current original-entry branch?

prioritize_independent_validation_plan_i: The exact H-A2.32 rule is preserved with no threshold change or OOS-selected filter.
Retained OOS performance does not collapse under leave-one-out or big-day checks.
The retained sample is not dominated by a single calendar/regime bucket beyond the disclosed diagnostic limits.
Skip-cost tradeoff remains directionally useful while still labeled under-sampled/underpowered.
run_another_local_diagnostic_if: A specific robustness failure appears explainable using existing local fields only.
The follow-up can be pre-registered without changing threshold 0.001 through OOS review.
The follow-up would clarify mechanism rather than optimize a new filter.
return_to_delayed_quote_acquisition_if: The original-entry rule is too weak without the 15-minute conflict component.
The economic logic requires waiting beyond 09:35 ET.
A delayed-entry quote/fill acquisition plan can be scoped without breaching cost or provider guardrails.
park_h_a2_original_entry_branch_if: Retained OOS performance is dominated by one or two days.
Regime/calendar concentration makes the signal non-generalizable under current evidence.
Skip-cost analysis shows the rule mainly hides losses through sample collapse.

allowed_claims: H-A2 has a pre-registered stricter original-entry robustness/prioritization review.
The review may use only existing local artifacts and the H-A2.32 09:35-only rule.
The future run may produce E1 diagnostic/prioritization evidence only.
The review may recommend whether independent validation-data planning is worth pursuing.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim H-A2 edge is validated.
Do not claim E2 acceptance-grade evidence.
Do not run exact replay from this preregistration.
Do not buy paid data from this preregistration.
Do not request IBKR bars from this preregistration.
Do not call LLMs from this preregistration.
Do not retry GDELT from this preregistration.
Do not change threshold 0.001 through OOS review.
Do not add a new OOS-selected filter.
future_run_completion_criteria: The future run preserves the H-A2.32 09:35-only rule and threshold 0.001.
The future run reports leave-one-out or big-day dependency status.
The future run reports regime/calendar concentration status.
The future run reports skip-cost tradeoff and sample-adequacy labels.
The future run caps claims at E1 and writes research log 032 if it completes as a real experiment.
tier_blockers: Future run can be E1 diagnostic/prioritization evidence only
